conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have one child, Alex, who is 6 years old and lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask:

Are you the person responsible for Alex? This means you're the main person caring for them - for example, you're the one who typically handles things like getting them ready for school, arranging childcare, making sure they eat properly, and generally looking after their day-to-day needs.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm the one who looks after Alex day-to-day - I get them ready for school, make their meals, take care of them when they're ill, all of that. I'm their main carer.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand your residency status. Are you currently living in the UK, and do any of the following apply to you:

- You're a UK national
- You have settled status (indefinite leave to remain)
- You have pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- You have another immigration status that gives you the right to claim benefits in the UK

Or are you from Ireland or another country with specific arrangements?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm living in the UK - I'm in England. And yes, I'm a UK national, so I have the right to claim benefits here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Just one more question to complete my assessment:

Is Alex currently in hospital or in local authority care for an extended period, or do they live with you full-time?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me full-time at home. They're not in hospital or in care or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

You meet all the key eligibility criteria:
- Alex is 6 years old (under 16)
- You are Alex's main carer and responsible for their day-to-day care
- Alex lives with you full-time
- You're a UK national living in the UK
- Alex is not in extended hospital or local authority care

You can now proceed to apply for Child Benefit. Please note that while you're eligible to claim, if you or your partner have an individual income over £50,000 per year, there is a High Income Child Benefit Charge that may apply, which means you might have to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit through your tax return. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant is Alex's main carer, responsible for their day-to-day care including getting them ready for school, making meals, and general childcare responsibilities. Alex lives with the claimant full-time in the UK (England). The claimant is a UK national living in the UK, which satisfies the residency and immigration status requirements. Alex is not in hospital or local authority care. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 6-year-old child Alex. The claimant meets all the key eligibility criteria: they are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, they are the main carer, they are a UK national living in the UK, and the child is not in extended hospital or local authority care.
